pageDescription: Calculate the critical point of a function

definition.title: Critical Point Definition
definition.description: A critical point occurs where f'(x) = 0 or f'(x) is undefined

classification.title: Point Classification
classification.description: Use second derivative test: f''(x) > 0 (min), f''(x) < 0 (max), f''(x) = 0 (inconclusive)

applications.title: Real-world Applications
applications.description: Critical points help optimize functions in economics, engineering, and physics

criticalWarningsList.notDiagnostic: This calculator provides critical point calculations only
criticalWarningsList.actual: Manual verification recommended for complex functions

importantNotesList: This calculator finds critical points where f'(x) = 0 or is undefined
Second derivative test helps classify critical points
Some functions may have no critical points
Complex functions may require manual verification

messages.enterInformation: Enter the function to find critical points
messages.resultMessage: Your critical point calculation results are shown below

disclaimer.title: Important Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: This calculator provides critical point calculations based on standard calculus methods. Results may require manual verification for complex functions or those with special conditions.

seekHelp.title: Mathematics Resources
seekHelp.content: For advanced calculus problems and critical point analysis, consult mathematics textbooks or academic resources on differential calculus and optimization theory.

errors.derivative: Please enter a valid first derivative
errors.secondDerivative: Please enter a valid second derivative

tooltips.function: The mathematical function for which to find critical points
tooltips.variable: The variable with respect to which derivatives are calculated
tooltips.derivative: First derivative of the function (optional - will be calculated if left blank)
tooltips.secondDerivative: Second derivative of the function (optional - will be calculated if left blank)

find_critical_points_of_singlevariable_o:
Find critical points of single-variable or multivariable functions by solving f′(x)=0 or ∂f/∂x=0 and
                ∂f/∂y=0. Identify where derivatives are zero or undefined.
enter_your_function_to_find_critical_poi: Enter your function to find critical points automatically
use_x_for_singlevariable_x_and_y_for_mul: (use x for single-variable, x and y for multivariable)
supported_sin_cos_tan_sqrt_log_ln_pi_e_6: Supported: +, -, *, /, ^, sin, cos, tan, sqrt, log, ln, pi, e
if_your_function_only_uses_x_it_finds_fx: • If your function only uses x, it finds f'(x) and solves f'(x) = 0
if_your_function_uses_x_and_y_it_finds_f:
• If your
                          function uses x and y, it finds ∂f/∂x, ∂f/∂y and solves both = 0
step_i_find_the_first_derivative_of_the_: Step I: Find the first derivative of the given function.
step_ii_set_the_first_derivative_equal_t: Step II: Set the first derivative equal to zero and solve.
hence_the_critical_points_of_the_given_f: Hence, the critical point(s) of the given function: x =
step_i_calculate_the_first_partial_deriv: Step I: Calculate the first partial derivative w.r.t "x"
step_ii_calculate_the_first_partial_deri: Step II: Calculate the first partial derivative w.r.t "y"
step_iii_set_both_partial_derivatives_eq: Step III: Set both partial derivatives equal to zero
hence_the_critical_points_of_the_given_f: Hence, the critical points of the given function are:
how_to_find_critical_points_step_by_step: How to Find Critical Points (Step by Step)
set_fx_0_and_solve_for_x_55: Set f'(x) = 0 and solve for x
find_points_where_fx_is_undefined_56: Find points where f'(x) is undefined
set_both_partial_derivatives_equal_to_ze: Set both partial derivatives equal to zero
solve_the_system_of_equations_simultaneo: Solve the system of equations simultaneously
check_for_points_where_partials_are_unde: Check for points where partials are undefined
the_function_has_one_critical_point_at_x: The function has one critical point at x = -2/3, where the derivative equals zero.
the_function_has_one_critical_point_at_4: The function has one critical point at (-4/3, 16/9) where both partial derivatives equal zero.
a_critical_point_is_a_point_in_the_domai:
A critical point is a point in the domain of a function where the derivative is either zero or
                        undefined. These points are candidates for local maxima, minima, or inflection points.
how_do_you_find_critical_points_100: How do you find critical points?
for_singlevariable_functions_find_fx_and:
For single-variable functions, find f'(x) and solve f'(x) = 0, plus check where f'(x) is
                        undefined. For multivariable functions, find all partial derivatives, set them equal to zero,
                        and solve the system simultaneously.
critical_points_help_identify_local_extr:
Critical points help identify local extrema (maxima and minima) of functions, which are
                        essential for optimization problems in calculus, economics, engineering, and many other fields.
whats_the_difference_between_critical_po: What's the difference between critical points and inflection points?
critical_points_occur_where_fx_0_or_is_u:
Critical points occur where f'(x) = 0 or is undefined, while inflection points occur where
                        f''(x) = 0 and the concavity changes. A point can be both critical and an inflection point.
this_tool_is_for_educational_use_only_ve:
This tool is for educational use only. Verify results for academic or
                professional work. Complex functions may require advanced techniques not covered by this simplified
                calculator.

                ∂f/∂y=0. Identify where derivatives are zero or undefined.
